session_note: S86 — playtest round-6 regression fixes (user + friend live playtest): blue fog back (S85 overrode S63 user tuning), hazard-ring stray lines from world origin, pooped/eaten action-gating holes (drag layer + bench dimension ungated), OS cursor visible. P5 user-pre-approved amendment: reorganize BACKLOG around core geometric-builder gameplay + forward roadmap. User approval verbatim: 'approved! also one more edit which i preapprove...'.
council_result: Standard 1-round Trident Strike (Grok-DISRUPTOR + Gemini-AUDITOR short-prompt, both responded). Key resolutions: bench gate CONCEDED->GROK (central dispatch-entry gate + deny-by-default policy map, not per-verb enumeration); drag-cancel SYNTHESIS (claim-outcome-keyed cancel, no local radius mirror -> Grok desync risk eliminated); ghost dot OVERRULED->GEMINI+CLAUDE 2.75v1.0 (kept, debuff-only); pointer-lock REJECTED; fog lock SYNTHESIS (LOCKED_DECISIONS.md + locking unit test). PRIME-AUDIT delta: R1 drag-cancel radius mirror replaced after glued-spark recurrence risk identified; probe surfaced bonus stuck-drag-after-bench bug (onUp swallowed while benched leaves AttractDrag alive) — folded into P3 scope.

priorities.label: S86 P1 — MEMORY_FOG_COLOR revert to 0x000000 + LOCK (user demanded black twice: S63 + round-6)
priorities.check_method: S86 P1 (b0f3913): constants.ts:142 MEMORY_FOG_COLOR 0x161b2e -> 0x000000 with the full knob history in the comment (S59 design -> S63 user black -> S85 regress -> S86 user black AGAIN + LOCK). Enforcement is layered because docs alone provably failed (S85 read the S59 note and restored over the S63 user tuning): LOCKED_DECISIONS.md NEW §14 states the rule (old design notes do not outrank newer user tuning; restore requires fresh same-session user ask) and src/constants.lock.test.ts asserts MEMORY_FOG_COLOR===0x000000 as a CI-blocking tripwire whose header explains a failure means a session is overriding a USER decision, not a bug. Verified: targeted vitest 52/52 (lock + exploredMemory + fogBrush + vision); e2e/fog.spec.ts greped for colour literals pre-PDR = none (probes sample unexplored points). Pushed to master -> CI/deploy lineage.

priorities.label: S86 P2 — hazard identity ring stray lines: moveTo before each arc segment (Pixi v8 path connects from world origin)
priorities.check_method: S86 P2 (8e328cd): drawHazardRing now moveTo()s to each dash-segment start (x+r*cos(start), y+r*sin(start)) before arc() — canvas-path semantics otherwise draw a connecting line from the current pen position (world origin on the freshly-cleared per-hazard Graphics) to the arc start; user round-6 screenshots showed exactly that stray line to BOTH ringed hazards (potato + hunter), which also pinpoints why S85 unit tests missed it (they covered only the pure segment/alpha helpers, not Graphics path behavior). Inter-dash chords killed by the same subpath isolation. Verified: tsc clean, hazardRing 3/3, bundle re-measured at this render-code commit 546.6 KiB < 550 (S85 reflexion rule). Live no-stray-line screenshot deferred to the combined post-P4 preview pass (one server, all visual checks).
priorities.objective: Kill the line from (0,0) to each ringed hazard (screenshot-proven on potato + hunter) and inter-segment chords.
priorities.testing: vitest green; live screenshot of ringed hazard with no stray line

verification.needle: g.moveTo(x + radius * Math.cos(seg.start), y + radius * Math.sin(seg.start))

priorities.label: S86 P3 — central bench/debuff enforcement: dispatch-entry bench gate (deny-by-default policy map) + claim-outcome-keyed AttractDrag cancel + benched drag cancel
priorities.check_method: S86 P3 (4849393): TWO structural holes closed at the right layers. (1) Bench dimension: benchedUntilTick was input-layer-only — NEW benchGate.ts BENCH_INTENT_POLICY (explicit allow/deny per CLIENT_INTENT_TYPE, completeness locked by test vs protocol allowlist = Grok verb-drift defense) consulted at dispatch() ENTRY before any case body, so local input, optimistic joiner prediction AND host-applied remote intents all reject identically (pure fn of synced fields); deny=9 acquisitive/structural/offensive verbs, allow=UPDATE_AVATAR_POS+2 release-only drops (blocking a drop could strand Carrying). (2) Drag layer: gesture ENTRY now claim-outcome-keyed in onDown (dispatch synchronous locally on host AND joiner via PREDICTABLE_ACTIONS — verified main.ts:268 includes PICKUP_SPARK — so 'claim landed' = spark Carried-by-me right after dispatchFn; rejected claim -> no gesture/no capture/spark untouched) which kills the pooped full-speed haul at the source without any radius mirror (Council SYNTHESIS — R1 mirror design discarded after glued-spark desync risk); applyPerSubstep benched check kills in-flight gestures the catch orphaned AND the probe-discovered stuck-gesture-after-bench hole (onUp early-returns while input-locked), with defensive DROP_SPARK mirroring onUp/onLostCapture. Diagnostics: actorBenched bucket added (worldTypes+init+debugOverlay+both match-boundary resets — which also now clear pickupPoopedTooFar, leaking since S84). Verified: tsc clean; benchGate suite 11/11 first run; FULL vitest 1305/1305 (no regression from dispatch-entry gate or onDown reorder). Live pooped/eaten smoke deferred to combined post-P4 preview pass.
priorities.objective: Eaten players cannot collect/build (reducer-level, covers remote intents); pooped players cannot haul Free sparks at full cursor speed via the ungated drag lerp; stuck-drag-after-bench killed. All checks pure fns of synced fields.
priorities.scope: src/state/world.ts (dispatch gate), new bench-policy map module, src/input/controls.ts (drag cancel), tests
priorities.testing: vitest per gate + policy-map completeness test vs protocol action tables; existing 1293 stay green; live smoke pooped+eaten scenarios

priorities.label: S86 P4 — hide OS cursor over canvas during PLAYING (avatar IS the pointer) + faint local ghost dot only while pooped/benched
priorities.check_method: S86 P4 (53b4251): shouldHideOsCursor (PLAYING-only) drives BOTH app.renderer.events.cursorStyles.default AND a direct style write — the preview pass caught the real bug in the naive design: Pixi's EventSystem owns canvas.style.cursor and re-applies cursorStyles[mode] per pointer interaction, so a one-shot style write got clobbered (title-button hover left 'pointer' stuck over the live board); switching Pixi's OWN default mode survives every re-derivation (verified live: 'none' persists across pointermoves on the board, 'inherit' on title). Ghost ring: avatarRenderer.pointerGhost drawn at controls.cursor ONLY while shouldShowPointerGhost (benched || isCruiserDebuffed || residual poopedCursorTarget) — verified at OBJECT level (16px 2-instruction Graphics at cursor while pooped; cleared when healed). P3 live smoke same pass: pooped-far grab dispatches + rejects (bucket +1) + NO gesture; pooped-arrived grab lands (AttractDrag/Carried); eaten-mid-drag gesture dies + defensive drop + new grabs input-locked. Harness artifacts diagnosed honestly (letterbox css mapping; spatial-grid one-frame lag; fsmDrop player reconstruction staleing cached refs) — none were product bugs. ALSO fixed: 3 tsc errors in benchGate.test.ts that rode the P3 push (esbuild-vitest is type-blind; piped exit code masked tsc — S85 pipe lesson re-learned, re-ran unpiped). vitest 1312/1312; tsc exit 0 unpiped; bundle 548.3<550; zero console errors.
priorities.objective: cursor:none on canvas during PLAYING only (single derived visibility fn); title/lobby/overlays keep normal cursor; ghost dot local-only while avatar!=cursor.
priorities.scope: src/main.ts or render layer, src/render (ghost dot), tests where pure
priorities.testing: vitest; preview screenshot PLAYING (no OS cursor artefacts) + title (cursor back); bundle <=550 KiB re-measured (render code)

The roadmap is GROUNDED in an empirical combo-system audit run this session: combos.ts has 36 ordered pairs, 24 are literal 'Functional placeholder — generic bond' (incl. the user-named Dot→Square and Line→Circle), areaMultiplier is consumed by ZERO production code, isMagical is consumed by ZERO production code outside combos.ts (magic bonds score identically to placeholders — S84's +0.25 complexity applies to ALL bonds), discovery exists only as the title Codex with no in-match celebration. Tiers: T1 USER-MANDATED core game (G1a isMagical scoring premium; G1b behavior archetypes ECONOMY/DEFENSE/MOTION with determinism constraint; G2 family-traits + 2-4 placeholder promotions starting with the user-named pairs; G3 discovery toast+codex silhouettes; G4 build-feel juice) · T2 playtest loop (round 7 checklist + non-builder-win watch) · T3 infra with HONEST origin labels + host-migration explained in plain English (user asked 'what does that even mean') · T4 user-deferred VFX. Session protocol locked: regressions jump queue, otherwise lead Tier-1, no Claude/Council idea enters T1-3 without user sign-off (PARKED list), roadmap updated each close. S86 history entry written; verbatim user mandate quoted at top.
priorities.objective: BACKLOG.md rewritten: every item traced to USER-ASK vs CLAUDE-SUGGESTED, prioritized by gameplay impact (geometry/builder depth first), infra items (host-migration etc.) explained in plain language + ranked honestly, multi-session roadmap section.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #user-tuning-outranks-design-notes #lock-with-a-test-not-a-comment: S85 restored the 'designed' dim fog tier over the explicit S63 user tuning because the old design note read as a carry-forward while the user decision read as a mere knob — and the round-6 playtest caught it as a regression ('the stupid blue fog is back'). A documented decision provably failed to protect itself within 22 sessions; the fix layers LOCKED_DECISIONS §14 (rule: old design notes NEVER outrank newer user tuning; restores need a fresh same-session user ask) with constants.lock.test.ts, a CI tripwire whose failure message says 'you are overriding a USER decision'. LESSON: user-tuned values get a test-enforced lock at the moment of tuning, not a comment.

P2 #unit-test-the-drawing-not-just-the-math: the S85 hazard ring shipped with green unit tests (pure segment/alpha helpers) while the actual Graphics path drew a line from the WORLD ORIGIN to every ringed hazard — canvas-path semantics connect the pen to arc() start, and a freshly-cleared Graphics pen sits at (0,0). The user's screenshots were the detection. LESSON: when a feature's output surface is a Graphics path, pure-math helper tests are necessary but NOT sufficient — verify at the path/screenshot level (moveTo per dash; live screenshot now in the loop).

P3 #enforce-at-the-choke-point #the-gesture-is-a-verb-too: two consecutive sessions gated VERBS (S84 claim, S85 build) while the exploit lived in (a) the continuous DRAG gesture — a gate-rejected claim left the spark Free and the ungated AttractDrag lerp hauled it at full cursor speed forever — and (b) the bench dimension, which had NO reducer enforcement at all (input-layer onDown only; the catch force-drop even handed the eaten player their spark back into the live drag). Fixes: bench policy enforced ONCE at dispatch() entry via an explicit per-intent allow/deny map test-locked against CLIENT_INTENT_TYPES (a new verb cannot ship unclassified), and gesture ENTRY keyed on the CLAIM OUTCOME (dispatch is synchronous locally on host AND joiner via PREDICTABLE_ACTIONS — no local radius mirror to desync). LESSON: enumerate ALL consumers of a resource INCLUDING continuous gestures, and enforce actor-state locks at the dispatch choke point, not per-verb.

P4 #the-framework-owns-the-canvas-style #pipe-masking-recursed: Pixi v8's EventSystem re-applies cursorStyles[mode] on every pointer interaction, so the naive canvas.style.cursor='none' was silently clobbered by a button-hover 'pointer' — the durable switch is Pixi's OWN cursorStyles.default, and ONLY the live preview pass could reveal it. Separately the S85 pipe lesson RECURSED: 'tsc | head' returned the pipe's exit code, the P3 commit claimed 'tsc clean' for a tree whose NEW test file had 3 type errors (esbuild-vitest is type-blind), and CI's deploy build went RED until the P4 commit fixed them. LESSON: gates run unpiped with explicit exit capture — violated twice now, so check_method MUST quote the captured exit code, not assert cleanliness.

P5 #ground-roadmaps-in-code-audits #label-idea-origins: the user's frustration ('the whole point is being a geometric builder game and we have least focused on that') converted into an unarguable roadmap with three greps: 24 of 36 combos are literal 'Functional placeholder' entries (incl. the user-named Dot→Square and Line→Circle), isMagical has ZERO production consumers (magic bonds score the same as placeholders), areaMultiplier is dead data. A roadmap argued from measured gaps beats one argued from vibes; labeling every item USER-ASK vs CLAUDE/COUNCIL-SUGGESTED with a PARKED-until-sign-off rule structurally prevents the backlog from refilling with self-generated infra ahead of the user's game.

analyze_retrospective.mode: compact inline (GREEN ~35% — ZERO self-retrospective + Council CHECK calibration)
analyze_retrospective.method_effectiveness: Diagnose-before-PDR carried the session: all four regressions were root-caused from code + the user's screenshots BEFORE the Council round (the origin-line bug was identified from screenshot geometry alone — lines radiating from screen top-left = world origin = missing moveTo). The Standard 1-round Trident Strike earned its cost: Grok's central-gate challenge (CONCEDED) is structurally better than the per-verb plan, and his desync risk killed a flawed R1 drag-cancel design before it shipped (PRIME-AUDIT replaced it with claim-outcome keying). The live preview pass caught the one real bug no static gate could (Pixi cursor-style ownership) AND executed the full pooped/eaten exploit matrix through the real input path.
analyze_retrospective.check_calibration: CHECK Triumvirate: RALPH PASS · Gemini CONDITIONAL PASS (sole condition = hazard-ring evidence it was not shown; evidence exists in-session: 8e328cd hunk + live screenshot — condition satisfied) · Grok FAIL with 4/4 findings REJECTED on file/line evidence (lerp-after-return misread control flow; moveTo-angle misread the code; the optimistic-window claim is the inherent bounded self-healing prediction window and his own repro contradicted itself; same-frame toggle has no observable defect). Verdict SHIP at 2/3. Grok CHECK false-alarm rate this session: 4/4 — calibration row for the rolling window; his PLAN-phase contribution was simultaneously the session's most valuable adoption, so the lens split (PLAN strong / CHECK noisy) is the signal to track.
analyze_retrospective.process_failures_owned: (1) The S85 'never pipe a gate' lesson recursed — tsc piped through head reported the pipe's exit code, 3 test-file type errors rode the P3 push, CI deploy went RED until P4 fixed them (owned in the P4 commit message). (2) The first GROK-ANALYST CHECK call shipped with the diff MISSING after 'THE COMPLETE PRODUCTION DIFF:' — the exact S84 vacuous-review failure — but the S84-derived guard instruction worked: Grok refused to verdict instead of hallucinating; re-sent with hunks embedded. (3) Three preview-harness false alarms (letterbox css mapping, spatial-grid frame lag, fsmDrop stale player refs) cost ~4 eval rounds before the real assertions ran — none were product bugs, all diagnosed rather than papered over.
analyze_retrospective.wins: 4 user-visible regressions fixed + LIVE on spark-online.space same session (P4 E2E+Deploy SUCCESS); eaten/pooped enforcement is now structural (choke-point + test-locked policy) rather than whack-a-verb; the fog decision can no longer silently regress; the backlog is a user-first roadmap grounded in a measured combo-system audit; bonus fixes: pickupPoopedTooFar reset leak (since S84) + stuck-gesture-after-bench hole (found by probe, not playtest).
analyze_retrospective.session_cost: Generative $0. Council API: Grok 3 calls + Gemini 2 calls (~$0.06). 5 commits (b0f3913, 8e328cd, 4849393, 53b4251, cdcf4b5), 5/5 priorities, vitest 1299→1312, bundle 548.3<550 KiB, CI green on tip.

session_status: S86 COMPLETE: 5/5 priorities shipped + pushed, CI green on 53b4251 lineage (P3 deploy red explained + fixed by P4), CHECK Triumvirate SHIP (2/3; Grok 4/4 findings refuted on evidence), live site carries all fixes — awaiting playtest round 7
